JUDGE JAMES L. WARREN, REPORTER CHRISTINA PAXTON CSR #1558.

LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT AMERICAN & FOREIGN INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO STATE MORE SPECIFICALLY "THE SUBSTANCE OF EACH POLICY PROVISION ESSENTIAL TO THE PARTICULAR COVERAGE THAT THE INSURED CLAIMS." DART INDUSTRIES V. COMMERCIAL UNION INS. CO., 28 CAL.4TH 1059, 1071 (2002). ALTHOUGH DART ADDRESSED WHAT MUST BE PROVED, AS OPPOSED TO WHAT MUST BE PLEADED, IT IS APPROPRIATE TO REQUIRE THE COMPLAINT TO ALLEGE SOME SUBSTANCE AGAINST WHICH THE INSURERS ALLEGED DUTY CAN BE MEASURED. THE MERE CONCLUSION THAT THE POLICY COVERED THE UNDERLYING CLAIMS IS INSUFFICIENT. THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT, BUT RESPONDING PARTY SHOULD PLEAD FACTS BEYOND THE ALLEGATION OF BREACH OF THE COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING TO SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL A. KAREN CSR#8189

For Plaintiff American & Foreign'S Demurrer And Motion To Strike Defendant D/C Distribution'S Cross Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-04-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT AMERICAN & FOREIGN INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO STATE MORE SPECIFICALLY "THE SUBSTANCE OF EACH POLICY PROVISION ESSENTIAL TO THE PARTICULAR COVERAGE THAT THE INSURED CLAIMS." DART INDUSTRIES V. COMMERCIAL UNION INS. CO., 28 CAL.4TH 1059, 1071 (2002). ALTHOUGH DART ADDRESSED WHAT MUST BE PROVED, AS OPPOSED TO WHAT MUST BE PLEADED, IT IS APPROPRIATE TO REQUIRE THE COMPLAINT TO ALLEGE SOME SUBSTANCE AGAINST WHICH THE INSURERS ALLEGED DUTY CAN BE MEASURED. THE MERE CONCLUSION THAT THE POLICY COVERED THE UNDERLYING CLAIMS IS INSUFFICIENT. THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT, BUT RESPONDING PARTY SHOULD PLEAD FACTS BEYOND THE ALLEGATION OF BREACH OF THE COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING TO SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES. (301/PJB/AC) REPORTER: CAROL A. KAREN, CSR# 8189
